title: Catalog Studio View Lifecycle: add rename/delete helpers with semantic-model impact analysis
description:
Add the pure lifecycle operations for renaming and deleting views, including semantic-model dependency analysis and safe blocking rules.

Scope:
- Implement helper operations equivalent to `analyze_view_dependencies(draft, view_ref)`, `build_proposed_delete_view_text(draft, view_ref)`, and `build_proposed_rename_view_text(draft, view_ref, new_name, new_schema=None)`.
- Report semantic-model dependencies for `base_view` and `joins[].to_view` references.
- Block delete when dependent semantic-model references exist instead of auto-deleting or rewriting semantic models.
- Support rename by updating semantic-model references atomically when the mapping is unambiguous.
- Block rename when unqualified references are ambiguous or when the target `(schema, name)` already exists.
- Preserve reference style where practical: keep schema-qualified references qualified and keep unqualified references unqualified when they remain unambiguous.

Acceptance evidence:
- Delete helper returns a blocked dependency report for referenced views and proposed raw text for safe deletes.
- Rename helper updates semantic-model references when safe and blocks ambiguous or duplicate-target renames.
- No helper operation writes files directly.

## ExecPlan Reference

title: Catalog Studio View Lifecycle: add create/duplicate pages and safety-service workflow

title: Shrink CLI orchestration and rename the dashboard build flow
description:

Concrete steps:
- Extract filesystem helpers into `src/duckalog/cli_filesystem.py`, import-graph helpers into `src/duckalog/cli_imports.py`, and table/interactive-display helpers into `src/duckalog/cli_display.py`.

- Rename the dashboard controller and route flow from `/build` to `/run` across `src/duckalog/dashboard/routes/query.py`, `src/duckalog/dashboard/routes/home.py`, `src/duckalog/dashboard/components/layout.py`, `src/duckalog/dashboard/routes/__init__.py`, `src/duckalog/dashboard/app.py`, and `tests/test_dashboard.py`.
- Keep the dashboard rename internally consistent rather than carrying both old and new names at once.

Acceptance:
- `grep -Rno --exclude-dir=__pycache__ '/build\|BuildController\|build-status' src/duckalog/dashboard 2>/dev/null` shows only deliberate migration comments, if any.
- The CLI helper tests still pass after the helpers are extracted.
- If the `ui` extra is installed locally, the dashboard test suite also passes.
- Run:

title: Advanced dashboard, architecture, security, and migration docs refresh
description:
## Goal

Refresh the advanced documentation areas so dashboard, explanation, architecture, security, dependency-injection, and migration pages match current Duckalog behavior and the new Zensical site structure.

Preserve useful architectural context and ADRs while removing stale references to old commands or old docs topology. Ensure cross-links point to canonical pages selected by the documentation map.

- Advanced users can find current dashboard behavior, architecture context, security guidance, and migration notes without relying on stale README-only reference blocks.
- Dashboard and architecture docs link to canonical reference and explanation pages.
- Security and migration pages are visible in the Zensical navigation or reachable from appropriate index pages.
- No advanced docs page knowingly duplicates another page without explaining its distinct purpose.
## Shared Acceptance Criteria

- `uv run zensical build` succeeds from the repository root and writes `site/index.html` when this issue's slice is complete enough to build.
- The generated site preserves the intended navigation for the pages touched by this issue.

title: Catalog Studio Form Editor: add structured view proposed-text helpers
description:
Implement pure helper functions that transform structured view form submissions into proposed raw YAML/JSON text.

Scope:
- Implement an operation equivalent to `build_proposed_view_config_text(draft, original_name, values) -> str`.
- Accept form values for `original_name`, `name`, `db_schema`, `description`, `tags`, `definition_kind`, `sql`, `sql_file_path`, `sql_template_path`, `sql_file_variables`, `source`, `uri`, `database`, `table`, `catalog`, and `options`.
- Convert comma-separated tags to stripped non-empty lists.

title: Catalog Studio editing safety: add backup, atomic save, and reload verification

- Ensure invalid proposals never create a backup or mutate the target file.

- Every successful save creates a readable backup sibling.

title: Official Zensical toolchain and CI cutover
description:
## Goal

Make Zensical the official Duckalog documentation build path in CI and maintainer docs, then remove obsolete MkDocs assumptions when safe.

## Work

Change `.github/workflows/tests.yml` so documentation-only pull requests and pushes run documentation validation, either by removing `**.md` and `docs/**` from `paths-ignore` or by adding a dedicated docs workflow. Replace MkDocs installation/build commands with project dependency setup and `uv run zensical build`. The Zensical build must be a hard CI gate: do not use `|| echo`, `continue-on-error`, or other failure-swallowing patterns.

Update `pyproject.toml` and `uv.lock` so Zensical and the mkdocstrings package required by Zensical are in the dev dependency group. Remove `mkdocs`, `mkdocs-material`, and `mkdocstrings[python]` only after Zensical build, API rendering, snippet expansion, Mermaid rendering, search, and navigation are proven. Update `docs/documentation-duplication-policy.md` so the validation checklist references Zensical, not MkDocs. Keep or delete `mkdocs.yml` according to the validated compatibility decision.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Documentation CI runs on docs-only changes.
- CI uses `uv run zensical build` as a hard failure gate and uploads `site/`.
- Maintainer instructions no longer say `mkdocs build` is the canonical docs check.
- `pyproject.toml` and `uv.lock` agree on final docs dependencies.
- `docs/documentation-duplication-policy.md` documents Zensical validation and README/snippet behavior.
## Shared Acceptance Criteria

- `uv run zensical build` succeeds from the repository root and writes `site/index.html` when this issue's slice is complete enough to build.
- The generated site preserves the intended navigation for the pages touched by this issue.
- Zensical build warnings are inspected; no known internal Markdown links are left broken by this issue.
- Any changes to shared README/docs intro content preserve the documented single-source mechanism or explicitly document the accepted replacement.

## ExecPlan Reference

- Plan: `.execflow/plans/duckalog-docs-zensical-framework/execplan.md`
- Milestone: 5 - Official toolchain cutover
- Read these sections before implementing:
  - "Context and Orientation" for repo layout and terms
  - Milestone 5 for this issue's scope
  - "Interfaces and Dependencies" for types and contracts
  - "Decision Log" for any decisions already made

## Scheduling Hints

- Kind: migration
- Depends on: m1, m2, m3, m4a, m4b
- Related to: All documentation refresh issues; this is the final official cutover.
- Conflicts with: Do not run concurrently with dependency/config edits in `pyproject.toml`, `uv.lock`, `.github/workflows/tests.yml`, `mkdocs.yml`, or `zensical.toml`.
- Parallel-safe with: none; perform after the docs slices and Zensical build foundation are validated
- Serialization points: CI workflow, docs dependency group, `mkdocs.yml` retirement, duplication policy
- Suggested worktree isolation: optional
close_reason: All acceptance criteria met. Zensical is now the official docs toolchain in CI and dev dependencies. MkDocs removed.

title: Reference, API, CLI, and config accuracy refresh
description:
## Goal

Refresh Duckalog reference documentation so CLI flags, Python entry points, configuration fields, and API examples match the current code and render under Zensical.

## Work

Validate CLI reference text with `uv run duckalog --help`, `uv run duckalog run --help`, and relevant command help. Validate public Python names against `src/duckalog/python_api.py` and `src/duckalog/connection.py`.

Important known corrections from the ExecPlan: `connect_to_catalog` accepts `database_path`, not `db_path`; `CatalogConnection` exposes `get_connection()` and `close()` plus context-manager support, not direct `execute()`, `rebuild_catalog()`, `is_ready()`, or `get_metadata()` methods. Confirm `docs/reference/api.md` renders through Zensical mkdocstrings or document an explicit accepted limitation with a fallback.

## Acceptance Criteria

- CLI reference examples match actual `duckalog --help` output.
- Hand-written API examples use real public methods and parameters.
- `docs/reference/api.md` renders API reference content through Zensical mkdocstrings or documents the fallback.
- Config schema reference links and examples remain valid under the new Zensical navigation.
## Shared Acceptance Criteria

- `uv run zensical build` succeeds from the repository root and writes `site/index.html` when this issue's slice is complete enough to build.
- The generated site preserves the intended navigation for the pages touched by this issue.

- Any changes to shared README/docs intro content preserve the documented single-source mechanism or explicitly document the accepted replacement.

title: Share one catalog-state pipeline and split secret SQL helpers
description:
Milestone 4 reduces runtime duplication in the engine and connection layers. The goal is to make one private catalog-state pipeline own settings, secrets, attachments, optional Iceberg catalogs, and view creation, then split the secret SQL generator into smaller provider-specific helpers without changing the modeled `scope` behavior.

Concrete steps:
- Introduce one internal helper in `src/duckalog/engine.py` that applies catalog state to a DuckDB connection.
- Make both `CatalogBuilder` and `CatalogConnection` call that shared helper instead of reassembling the sequence separately.
- Split `generate_secret_sql()` in `src/duckalog/sql_generation.py` into smaller private helpers for the provider-specific branches.
- Preserve the current `SecretConfig.scope` behavior so the existing scope-related SQL tests continue to pass.

Acceptance:
- `src/duckalog/connection.py` no longer replays the engine state-restoration sequence by hand.
- `tests/test_sql_generation.py` passes, including the scope-related cases.
- Run:

    uv run pytest tests/test_connection_api.py tests/test_engine_cli.py tests/test_engine_hierarchical.py tests/test_engine_remote_export.py tests/test_sql_generation.py -q

## ExecPlan Reference
